entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A major staple food crop for people in semi-arid areas of Africa and Asia.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where sorghum is a staple food and the study was conducted.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Continent where sorghum is a staple food.

entities.name: POST-FLOWERING DROUGHT
entities.description: A global constraint affecting the production of sorghum.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN
entities.description: Characteristics of sorghum varieties aimed for improvement to enhance drought tolerance.

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: Country where farmer-preferred sorghum varieties were improved.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique used to improve stay-green characteristics in sorghum.

entities.name: KASP MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used for genotyping individuals linked with STG 3A and STG 3B QTLs.

entities.name: STG 3A
entities.description: A specific quantitative trait locus linked with stay-green characteristics.

entities.name: STG 3B
entities.description: A specific quantitative trait locus linked with stay-green characteristics.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Regions of DNA associated with a particular phenotypic trait.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISM MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used to observe favourable alleles in BC2F1 populations.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per panicle.

entities.name: WATER IRRIGATION REGIMES
entities.description: Different levels of water application used to observe grain weight.

entities.name: GRAIN WEIGHT
entities.description: The mean weight of grain produced per panicle.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand drought conditions.
